message:
test(fcp-host): repair 34 pre-existing failures across 3 modules

All trace to production behavior changes that landed without updating
the corresponding tests. No production behavior is changed; only test
fixtures and stale assertions.

supply_chain (31 failures → 0):

  Both `valid_attestation()` and `valid_sbom()` test helpers used the
  literal placeholder string `"sig-placeholder"` for the
  `SupplyChainSignature.signature` field. The upstream validator
  (fcp-evidence / fcp-core::supply_chain) tightened to require the
  signature to parse as hex or base64; placeholder text trips the
  `attestation_validation` and `sbom_validation` steps with
  `signature must be valid hex or base64`, flipping the entire
  outcome to `Deny`.

  Replaced both helper signatures with a 128-character hex string (the
  shape of an Ed25519 signature). All 31 supply_chain tests under the
  default policy / dev-override / cache / SLSA / SBOM / digest paths
  flip back to `Allow` and pass.

  No production code changed.

admin_state::capability_issuance_request_default_ttl (1 → 0):

  Test deserializes a JSON payload with only `connector_id`, `zone_id`,
  `principal_id` and asserts the default TTL kicks in. The struct
  `CapabilityIssuanceRequest` gained a NEW required field
  `capability_id` (the live invoke verifier checks the operation's
  declared capability rather than its id, so issuance must stamp it
  too); serde now rejects the payload with `missing field
  "capability_id"` before any default fires. Added the field to the
  test JSON; assertion semantics on TTL/dry_run/max_delegation_depth
  unchanged.

enforcement (2 → 0):

  Two tests asserted the revocation step is reported as
  `"revocation_freshness"`. Production `RevocationCheck::name()`
  returns `"revocation"` (the sibling test `revocation_freshness_name`
  already pins this — the canonical name was renamed but only some
  callers were updated). Updated both expected strings to
  `"revocation"`. The function names still read as `*_revocation_freshness*`
  because the *condition* under test is the freshness window — but
  the produced `check_name` string is the canonical step id.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-host --lib → 3171/3171 pass.

files.patch:
@@ -7461,8 +7461,18 @@ mod tests {
 
     #[test]
     fn capability_issuance_request_default_ttl() {
+        // `capability_id` was added as a required field in
+        // CapabilityIssuanceRequest after the live invoke verifier
+        // started checking the operation's declared capability rather
+        // than just the operation id. The default-ttl test focuses on
+        // serde defaults for the TTL/dry_run/max_delegation_depth
+        // fields, but it still has to send a complete payload that
+        // satisfies the now-mandatory fields — otherwise serde fails
+        // with `missing field "capability_id"` before any default kicks
+        // in.
         let json = r#"{
             "connector_id": "test:saas:1.0.0",
+            "capability_id": "test.invoke",
             "zone_id": "z:work",
             "principal_id": "agent:test"
         }"#;

@@ -429,7 +429,13 @@ mod tests {
             signature: SupplyChainSignature {
                 algorithm: "ed25519".to_string(),
                 key_id: "key-001".to_string(),
-                signature: "sig-placeholder".to_string(),
+                // 64-byte Ed25519 signature shape, 128 hex chars. The
+                // upstream validator (fcp-evidence/-core) requires the
+                // `signature` field to parse as hex or base64; any free-
+                // form placeholder (e.g. "sig-placeholder") trips
+                // `attestation_validation`/`sbom_validation` with
+                // `signature must be valid hex or base64`.
+                signature: "f".repeat(128),
                 signed_fields: SUPPLY_CHAIN_ATTESTATION_SIGNED_FIELDS
                     .iter()
                     .map(|s| (*s).to_string())
@@ -463,7 +469,13 @@ mod tests {
             signature: SupplyChainSignature {
                 algorithm: "ed25519".to_string(),
                 key_id: "key-002".to_string(),
-                signature: "sig-placeholder".to_string(),
+                // 64-byte Ed25519 signature shape, 128 hex chars. The
+                // upstream validator (fcp-evidence/-core) requires the
+                // `signature` field to parse as hex or base64; any free-
+                // form placeholder (e.g. "sig-placeholder") trips
+                // `attestation_validation`/`sbom_validation` with
+                // `signature must be valid hex or base64`.
+                signature: "f".repeat(128),
                 signed_fields: SBOM_SIGNED_FIELDS
                     .iter()
                     .map(|s| (*s).to_string())
